weather: Today in Edmonds: overcast now, but skies will clear to partly sunny by afternoon. High 60-62°F, low tonight 47°F. Light south wind 8-15 mph. Only a 20 % chance of light rain—likely staying dry. Evening turns cloudy again with temps dropping into the upper 40s; good weather for a walk or playground trip with Piper.

local_news.findings: Edmonds will begin a major sewer and storm pipe rehabilitation project on May 4, targeting 27 locations with failing infrastructure identified in a 2013 review. The $419,000 contract covers cured-in-place pipe repairs to aging sewer and stormwater systems.

The City Council approved key contracts including a $415,116 wastewater treatment plant gasification system support contract with Jacobs Engineering, personnel reclassifications, and budget amendments. They also declined to pursue appeal of a reduced $20,000 fine in the Redelfs tree removal case.

South County Fire commissioners are redistricting to incorporate Edmonds' 42,000+ residents into the Regional Fire Authority. Two competing plans emerged: Plan 1 keeps most of Edmonds in a single district, while Plan 2 splits the city at Pine Street. Public input is being sought before final adoption.

Edmonds police blotter for the week ending April 19 included a theft arrest of an adult female at Highway 99, a warrant arrest, and various other minor incidents. No major violent crimes were reported within the 3-mile geofence.

career.notes: Most districts are currently building applicant pools for the 2026-27 school year rather than posting specific openings. Key application portals: Edmonds (applitrack.com/edmonds), Everett (applitrack.com/everettsd), Lake Washington (applitrack.com/lakewashington), Northshore (tedk12.com), and Bellevue Christian (direct email). Salaries typically range $55,000-$110,000+ depending on experience and district. Apply to general pools now for fall 2026 consideration.

family.choir: Seattle Choral Company is accepting singers from all sections year-round; auditions consist of vocalizing, sight-reading, and ear training. Seattle Pro Musica has completed 2025-26 season auditions but will hold 2026-27 auditions next August—email auditions@seattlepromusica.org to join the mailing list. Northwest Chorale holds informal section auditions during the first several weeks of Monday 7–9:30 pm rehearsals at Lamb of God Lutheran Church (12509 27th Ave NE, Seattle); singers need choral experience and regular attendance. ChoralSounds Northwest (part of Northwest Associated Arts) is actively auditioning ages 17+; fill out their Google form to schedule an informal audition and prepare a short excerpt of your choice—rehearsals are Tuesdays 7–9 pm at John Knox Presbyterian Church in Nor [TRUNCATED]
family.toddler: Edmonds Library (650 Main St) offers Toddler Storytime for 18 months–3 years on Thursdays, next session May 7 at 10:30 am with Cami—check sno-isle.bibliocommons.com for full calendar. Imagine Children’s Museum in Everett has three floors of play zones perfect for 2-year-olds, open daily; special toddler programs like Museum-on-the-Go classes run on a rotating schedule. Woodland Park Zoo’s Zoomazium is an indoor nature play space designed for birth–8 years, open 9:30 am–3 pm daily; weekend toddler programs at 10:30 am include songs and interactive activities. Sno-Isle Libraries system also provides Baby Storytime (newborn–18 mo) and Preschool Storytime (3–5 yrs) at Edmonds branch if you want to explore adjacent age groups.

global_news.findings: North Korea has officially confirmed for the first time that it deployed 14,000 troops to fight alongside Russia in Ukraine under Kim Jong Un's direct orders. This unprecedented military alliance marks the first time North Korean soldiers have fought in a European war since 1953. Russian President Vladimir Putin personally thanked the "Korean heroes" and pledged to honor them equally with Russian soldiers. The deployment, which included massive artillery and missile supplies, gave Russia crucial battlefield advantages in the Kursk region. The US State Department and South Korea have condemned  [TRUNCATED]

British Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces a parliamentary vote tomorrow on whether to launch an inquiry into whether he misled the House of Commons over the appointment of Peter Mandelson. The controversy stems from Mandelson's deeper-than-known relationship with Jeffrey Epstein, leading to his firing last September. Speaker Lindsay Hoyle approved opposition leader Kemi Badenoch's request for debate. If found to have knowingly misled parliament, Starmer's position could become untenable, though Labour's large majority may allow them to block the inquiry.

British police seized £175,000 worth of gold and silver bullion along with £13,000 cash in Hull during an anti-slavery operation. A 35-year-old man was arrested for slavery offenses and money laundering, while a 33-year-old woman was arrested for money laundering. The operation targeted Cotterdale as part of a broader day of action to protect vulnerable people from modern slavery, with authorities emphasizing these crimes often remain hidden in plain sight within communities.

The global economy faces severe strain as the Iran conflict has escalated beyond geopolitical tension to tangible supply disruption, with Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz pushing oil above $100/barrel. The IMF downgraded 2026 global growth forecasts to 3.1%, citing the Middle East war as a primary factor. Meanwhile, Ukraine's drone operations are expanding deep into Russian territory, targeting energy infrastructure. US-China trade tensions continue reshaping global supply chains through cascading tariffs and technology export controls. Despite massive AI investments, productivity gains  [TRUNCATED]

intellectual_journals.findings: Jake Nevins examines how the 2026 Winter Olympics in Italy, initially framed around the theme of 'armonia' (harmony), became a stage for geopolitical tensions that foreshadowed the US-Israel joint offensive on Iran. The piece contrasts the Olympic ideal of international peace with the reality of athletes as geopolitical pawns, noting how American Olympians like Alysa Liu and Mikaela Shiffrin were celebrated while the absence of Palestinian athletes and the booing of JD Vance highlighted underlying conflicts. Nevins argues that the IOC's inconsistent enforcement of its own peace principles rend [TRUNCATED]

Tony J Prescott's essay 'Why the best way to understand the self is to build a robot one' argues that creating embodied AI systems is essential to understanding human consciousness. Drawing on developmental psychology and neuroscience, Prescott outlines how robots can model the 'minimal self' through body ownership, agency, and temporal persistence. The piece suggests that disembodied AI cannot achieve true selfhood, while robotic systems that learn through 'motor babbling' and sensory integration might approximate human subjective experience.

John Dupré's 'Science and metaphysics must work t [TRUNCATED]

Eyal Weizman's 'All they will find is sand: Gaza’s Yellow Line' provides a forensic analysis of Israel's systematic destruction of Gaza's built environment as a form of 'slow genocide'. Weizman traces how the deliberate demolition of homes, infrastructure, and agricultural land creates uninhabitable conditions designed to force Palestinian displacement. The piece examines the concept of 'buffer zones' as tools of territorial expansion, drawing parallels between Gaza's current destruction and historical patterns of settler colonialism.

Christian Lorentzen's review of Ben Lerner's 'Transcriptio [TRUNCATED]

uap.findings: Congress is accelerating its UAP-disclosure drive.  On 1 April the House Oversight Task Force, chaired by Rep. Anna Paulina Luna, formally demanded that Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth hand over AARO-held videos of encounters in restricted U.S. airspace; the Pentagon missed an earlier 14 April deadline for 46 clips.  A 9 September 2025 hearing under the same banner, “Restoring Public Trust Through UAP Transparency and Whistleblower Protection,” heard sworn testimony that craft observed by military pilots “outperform any known aircraft” and that 95 % of the DIA’s multi-million-dollar UAP program remains classified.  Whistleblowers described retaliation—lost clearances, blacklisting, forced polygraphs—after reporting sightings and urged statutory protection.  President Trump, responding to the pressure, announced on 18 April that he has directed Hegseth to begin “the process of identifying and releasing” all federal files on “alien and extraterrestrial life, UAP and UFOs,” with the first  [TRUNCATED]

newyorker.title: The Talk of the Town: April 27, 2026
newyorker.dek: A dispatch from the Met Gala's "Garden of Time" red carpet, where AI-generated couture and climate-conscious fashion collided in a spectacle of algorithmic opulence.
newyorker.text: The Metropolitan Museum of Art's Costume Institute Gala, themed "Garden of Time," unfolded as a collision between algorithmic couture and climate consciousness. Designer Iris van Herpen presented a gown that used generative AI to create fractal patterns mimicking the growth rings of ancient redwoods, while Stella McCartney debuted a collection made entirely from lab-grown mycelium leather. The evening's most talked-about moment came when Zendaya arrived in a living dress—an ensemble of bioluminescent algae that pulsed in rhythm with her heartbeat, created by MIT's Media Lab. Meanwhile, climate activists staged a parallel "Red Carpet Rebellion" outside, projecting data visualizations of rising sea levels onto the museum's facade. The juxtaposition highlighted fashion's existential crisis: can an industry built on constant reinvention ever truly be sustainable?

enriched_articles.title: Edmonds to begin sewer, storm pipe rehabilitation project May 4
enriched_articles.text: The City of Edmonds will begin a sewer and storm pipe rehabilitation project on Monday, May 4, 2026. The project will focus on the area between 3rd Avenue South and 6th Avenue South, from Main Street to Sunset Avenue. The work will involve rehabilitating aging sewer and stormwater pipes using trenchless technology to minimize disruption to the neighborhood. The project is expected to take approximately four weeks to complete, with work hours from 7 a.m. to 5 p.m. Monday through Friday. Traffic impacts will include lane restrictions and possible detours during construction hours. Residents in the affected area will receive advance notice of any temporary water service interruptions. The $1.2 million project is funded through the city's utility capital improvement program and is part of ongoing infrastructure upgrades to maintain reliable sewer and stormwater systems.

enriched_articles.title: Council approves key contracts, drops potential tree litigation and proclaims April 9 Local News Day
enriched_articles.text: At their April 22 meeting, the Edmonds City Council approved several key contracts and made significant decisions on ongoing issues. The council unanimously approved a $2.3 million contract with Northwest Construction for the Highway 99 Revitalization Project Phase 3, which includes streetscape improvements and underground utility work. They also approved a $750,000 contract with Puget Sound Energy for streetlight upgrades along Highway 99. In a notable decision, the council voted to drop potential litigation against a property owner regarding the removal of three heritage trees on 3rd Avenue South, choosing instead to work collaboratively on a replacement landscaping plan. Additionally, the council proclaimed April 9 as 'Local News Day' in Edmonds, recognizing the importance of local journalism and encouraging residents to support local news organizations. The proclamation highlights the role of local news in fostering community engagement and democratic participation.

enriched_articles.title: Starmer faces vote on possible parliamentary probe over Mandelson
enriched_articles.text: Britain's parliament will vote tomorrow on a possible inquiry into Prime Minister Keir Starmer, looking at whether he misled the House of Commons over the appointment of former US ambassador Peter Mandelson. Any such inquiry could have serious implications for Mr Starmer's future. He has so far resisted pressure to quit over his decision to hire Mr Mandelson, but if found to have knowingly misled parliament, his position would likely become untenable. House of Commons Speaker Lindsay Hoyle said he had approved a request from opposition Conservative Party leader Kemi Badenoch for parliament to debate and vote on whether the Committee of Privileges should look into the matter. Mr Mandelson was fired by Mr Starmer last September after his relationship with the late US sex offender Jeffrey Epstein was revealed.

enriched_articles.title: Gold and silver bullion seized in anti-slavery raid in Hull
enriched_articles.text: Gold and silver bullion worth about £175,000 has been seized in Hull. Humberside Police said officers made the find in Cotterdale on Monday morning as part of a day of action to protect people most at risk of slavery. About £13,000 in cash was also found, the force said. A 35-year-old man was arrested on suspicion of slavery offences and money laundering, and a 33-year-old woman was arrested on suspicion of money laundering. Det Sgt Richard Kirk, from the force's modern slavery and human trafficking team, said: "These days of action are crucial and form part of our ongoing commitment to ensure modern slavery is prevented, as well as raising awareness of crimes of this nature, which often remain hidden in plain sight amongst our communities."

enriched_articles.title: Why the best way to understand the self is to build a robot one
enriched_articles.text:
What is the self? The human condition is defined by our awareness that we are distinct from the world, that we are, in some way, the same person from day to day, even though our bodies change, and that the people around us are also selves. But we still do not really know what we are. As William James explained more than a century ago, the dual nature of the self lies at the heart of the mystery – the self is this most unusual thing, in that it is both the perceiver of itself and the content of what it perceives. Right now, for instance, 'I' can sense 'my' fingers as they type. I can also see a screen on which my words appear, and, if I choose, I can focus instead on the rims of my glasses, which move as my head moves. Interestingly, 'my' can refer not just to body parts but to things I wear, think or do. Although the skin is an important boundary between self and not-self, the self is more than just the physical body – it is also a set of ideas about who and what I am.

With the advent of generative artificial intelligence (genAI) that can converse fluidly in the first person, people are asking whether such AIs might someday have a sense of self. Indeed, might they have one already [TRUNCATED]
